meaning: n.鑴氬皷,瓒惧皷vi.鐢ㄨ剼灏栬蛋
examples: He walks as lightly as possible, almost on tiptoe, in order not to wear out his heels in too short a time.
To her, the exercises that Pavlova and the other ballerinas were doing seemed painful, even harmful, standing on tiptoe for hours, moving their bodies in unnatural ways.
She had to stand on tiptoe to reach the top shelf.
Cappelli's side business works, in part, because he doesn't have to tiptoe around a boss.
Poulenc, for example, loved to tiptoe the line between the sublime and the silly.
